steps.title: Read signs from the line
steps.content: The line of best fit crosses the $y$-axis above the origin, so the intercept is positive. It also rises from left to right, so the slope is positive.

steps.title: Reject the negative-intercept choices
steps.content: Start by checking choices C and D. Both cross the $y$-axis at $-2.8$, below the origin. The displayed line crosses the $y$-axis above the origin, so C and D cannot match.

steps.title: Reject the negative-slope choice
steps.content: Now test choice B, $y=2.8-1.7x$. Its $y$-intercept is positive, but the line falls from left to right. The graph's best-fit line rises from left to right, so B is out.

steps.title: Confirm the matching model
steps.content: Choice A, $y=2.8+1.7x$, is the only option with both features: a positive $y$-intercept and a positive slope. It matches the graph.

steps.title: Confirm the model
steps.content: Choice A, $y=2.8+1.7x$, has both required graph features, so it is correct.
